\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Iterative Reduction}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.IterativeReduction
\section{Overview}

This module carries out Steps 8--9 of the LMN argument: it applies the one-step
switching/compression mechanism repeatedly across all intermediate circuit layers
(reducing depth $d$ down to depth $2$), and combines the per-stage failure
probabilities into a single union bound of order $s\cdot(1/2)^l$. The arithmetic
package shows that with $l=\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ the total failure probability is
at most $\varepsilon/2$.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{theorem}[Reduction at subsequent layers]
\lean{LMN.subsequent_step_reduction}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.one_step_reduction_failure_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Let $g_1,\dots,g_{s_i}$ be width-$\le l$ DNFs on $n$ variables (with $0<l$,
$0<n$), and let $0<p\le 1/(40l)$ with $p\le 1$. Under a Bernoulli$(p)$ random
restriction $\rho$, the probability that some restricted gate fails to be
representable by a width-$\le l$ CNF agreeing with it pointwise is at most
$s_i\cdot\bigl((1/2)^l+\exp(-np/3)\bigr)$. This is the one-step reduction bound
applied with width parameter $l$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Decision-tree depth at subsequent layers]
\lean{LMN.subsequent_step_dtDepth}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.one_step_dtDepth_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Under the same hypotheses, a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ leaves every one of
the $s_i$ width-$\le l$ gates with restricted function of decision-tree depth at
most $l$, with probability at least
$1-s_i\cdot\bigl((1/2)^l+\exp(-np/3)\bigr)$. This is the decision-tree-depth form
of the one-step bound applied with width $l$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Multi-stage failure bound]
\lean{LMN.multi_stage_failure_bound}
\leanok
If over $m$ stages each failure bound satisfies
$\mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le (\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot\alpha$ with
$\alpha\ge 0$, and $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$, then
$\sum_i \mathrm{failure\_bound}(i)\le s\cdot\alpha$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Union bound for restriction probabilities]
\lean{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_union_bound_fin}
\leanok
\uses{SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_nonneg'}
For $0\le p\le 1$ and finitely many events $A_0,\dots,A_{m-1}$ on restrictions, the
Bernoulli$(p)$ probability that some $A_i$ holds is at most the sum of the
individual probabilities:
\[
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(\exists i,\,A_i)\le
\sum_i \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,(A_i).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Iterative dominant-term bound]
\lean{LMN.iterative_dominant_term_bound}
\leanok
For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, setting
$l=\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)$ gives
\[
\sum_i \bigl(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\bigr)\cdot (1/2)^l \le \varepsilon/2 .
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Two-stage composed union bound]
\lean{LMN.two_stage_composed_union_bound}
\leanok
\uses{SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight}
For $0\le p\le 1$ and $0\le q\le 1$, and decidable events $A,B$ on restrictions,
\[
\sum_{\rho_1}\mathrm{bernoulliRestrWeight}\,p\,\rho_1\cdot
\mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,(A(\rho_1)\vee B(\rho_2))
\le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A + \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,q\,B,
\]
where the inner event combines $A$ on the first-stage restriction with $B$ on the
second-stage restriction. This bounds the failure of a composed two-stage
Bernoulli restriction by the sum of the two individual stage probabilities.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Abstract iterative reduction]
\lean{LMN.abstract_iterative_reduction}
\leanok
\uses{LMN.iterative_dominant_term_bound}
For $\sum_i \mathrm{layer\_size}(i)\le s$ with $0<s$ and $0<\varepsilon$, if each
per-stage bound satisfies
$\mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le(\mathrm{layer\_size}(i))\cdot(1/2)^{\log_2(2s/\varepsilon)}$,
then $\sum_i \mathrm{per\_stage\_bound}(i)\le\varepsilon/2$. This packages the
union bound over all $d-2$ iterations as a purely arithmetic consequence of the
per-stage bounds.
\end{theorem}
